data.title: Arcutis Biotherapeutics Director Sells Shares
data.description:
Keith R Leonard, a Director at Arcutis Biotherapeutics, sold 39,272 shares of Common Stock on March 2, 2026, under a 10b5-1 trading plan. The shares were sold at a weighted average price of $25.392, totaling $997,194. Following the transaction, Leonard directly owns 22,123 shares and indirectly owns 1,750 shares through the Leonard Family Trust. The sales were executed at prices ranging from $25.05 to $26.039.

Reporter NameLeonard Keith R
RelationshipDirector
TypeSell
Amount$997,194
SEC FilingForm 4

SEC Filing: Arcutis Biotherapeutics, Inc. [ ARQT ] - Form 4 - Mar. 04, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: US Judge Orders Trump Administration To Halt Tariff Calculations For Importers: Report
data.description:
A U.S. federal trade court judge has reportedly ordered the Trump administration to stop calculating tariffs on importers’ customs paperwork.

According to a report from Bloomberg, Judge Richard Eaton from the federal trade court in New York has ordered the move to simplify refunds.

The judge has also questioned the administration about the Customs and Border Protection continuing to charge U.S. President Donald Trump’s emergency tariffs on importers’ customs paperwork even after the Supreme Court struck down tariffs.

Get updates to this developing story directly on Stocktwits.

data.title: NNBR: Net sales fell 9.1% and net loss narrowed to $34.0 million, driven by cost actions and plant closures
data.description:
Net sales declined 9.1% to $422.2 million, with a narrowed net loss of $34.0 million as cost actions and plant closures improved operating results. The company refinanced its term loan, closed two facilities, and sold its Lubbock operations, while maintaining focus on cost structure and margin expansion amid macroeconomic headwinds.

Original document: NN, Inc. [NNBR] SEC 10-K Annual Report — Mar. 5 2026

Disclaimer

data.description:

data.title: Cracker Barrel's Q2 revenue falls
data.description:
Overview

  • Restaurant chain's fiscal Q2 revenue fell 7.9% yr/yr but beat analyst expectations

  • Reported adjusted EPS for fiscal Q2 of $0.25, compared to analyst expectations of a loss

  • Company updated fiscal 2026 outlook with revised revenue and EBITDA projections

Outlook

  • Cracker Barrel narrowed its fiscal 2026 revenue outlook at $3.24 bln to $3.27 bln

  • Company expects fiscal 2026 adjusted EBITDA of $85 mln to $100 mln

  • Cracker Barrel forecasts commodity inflation of 2.0% to 2.5% for fiscal 2026

Result Drivers

  • DECLINING COMPARABLE SALES - A 7.1% decrease in comparable restaurant sales and a 9.2% decrease in comparable retail sales dragged revenue

  • FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE ACTIONS - Co took additional actions to improve financial performance, aiming to regain prior momentum

data.title: Enhabit Q4 revenue rises
data.description:
Overview

  • Home health and hospice provider's Q4 revenue rose 4.7% yr/yr

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 beat analyst expectations

Outlook

  • Enhabit suspends financial guidance due to pending merger with Kinderhook

Result Drivers

  • NON-MEDICARE GROWTH - Enhabit reported a 16.0% increase in non-Medicare admissions, contributing to a 3.2% rise in home health net service revenue

  • HOSPICE CENSUS INCREASE - Hospice average daily census grew 9.9% year over year, boosting net service revenue by 10.0%

  • COST REDUCTION - Home health cost per patient day decreased 3.5% year over year, aiding margin improvement

data.title: American Eagle forecasts annual comparable sales growth above estimates
data.description:
American Eagle Outfitters forecast annual sales above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday, backed by marketing-driven demand for its apparel and accessories.

Shares of the company, which rose 58% in 2025, were up 5% after the bell.

The company's marketing efforts and new product launches aimed at attracting younger, higher‑earning shoppers have helped it offset the impact of the broader retail slowdown.

The company expects annual comparable sales to rise in the mid-single-digit percentage range, compared to analysts' estimates of a 2.92% rise, according to data compiled by LSEG.

data.title: Keros Therapeutics Q4 net loss narrows to $23.5 mln
data.description:
Overview

  • Biopharmaceutical firm reported narrowed Q4 net loss of $23.5 mln

  • Company reported FY net income of $87 mln for 2025, aided by Takeda license revenue

  • Keros plans Phase 2 trials for rinvatercept in DMD and ALS in 2026

Outlook

  • Keros expects to start Phase 2 trial for DMD in Q2 2026

  • Company plans to engage regulators for ALS trial design in H2 2026

  • Keros expects cash to fund operations into H1 2028

Result Drivers

  • TAKEDA LICENSE REVENUE - Net income for 2025 was largely driven by revenue from Keros' license agreement with Takeda

  • R&D EXPENSE DECREASE - Transition of elritercept-related R&D expenses to Takeda led to lower expenses

  • G&A EXPENSE INCREASE - Higher external costs led to increased general and administrative expenses despite reduced headcount
